walkthroughs.title: Get Started with SnapBack
walkthroughs.description: Learn the three protection levels and protect your first file in 2 minutes

steps.title: Three Levels of Protection
steps.description:
SnapBack gives you precise control with three protection levels:

**🧢 Watch** - Silent auto-snapshotting (no interruptions)

**👷 Warn** - Confirm before saving (safety prompt)

**⛑️ Block** - Required snapshot note (maximum protection)

Choose the right level for each file based on how critical it is.

media.markdown:
## When to Use Each Level

### 🧢 Watch - Most Files
- Configs you edit often
- Code you're actively working on
- Files where you want automatic safety

### 👷 Warn - Important Files
- Files that affect multiple systems
- Database migrations
- Build configurations

### ⛑️ Block - Critical Files
- `package.json`, `.env` files
- Authentication code
- Payment processing
- Anything that could break production

steps.title: Protect Your First File
steps.description:
Let's protect a file together:

1. Right-click any file in the Explorer
2. Hover over **SnapBack: Set Protection Level**
3. Choose **🧢 Watch Level** (silent mode)
4. The file now gets automatic snapshots when you save

[Open a file to try it now](command:workbench.action.quickOpen)

media.markdown:
## Protection Levels Menu

When you right-click a file, you'll see:

``````

**Pro Tip:** You can also use the Command Palette:
- Press `Ctrl+Shift+P` (or `Cmd+Shift+P` on Mac)
- Type "SnapBack: Protect File"
- Choose your protection level

steps.title: See Protection in Action
steps.description:
Now make a change to your protected file and save it.

✅ **Watch level** creates a snapshot silently
✅ Status bar shows confirmation briefly
✅ No interruptions to your flow

Notice how the file badge in the Explorer shows it's protected.

media.markdown:
## What Happens When You Save?

### 🧢 Watch Level
- Snapshot created automatically
- Brief status bar message
- No interruption

### 👷 Warn Level
- Confirmation dialog appears
- You can add a note (optional)
- Click "Save" to continue

### ⛑️ Block Level
- Dialog requires a note
- Note helps you remember why you made the change
- Cannot save without adding context

steps.title: Snap Back (Restore Snapshots)
steps.description:
Open the SnapBack sidebar to see all your snapshots:

1. Click the SnapBack icon in the Activity Bar (left sidebar)
2. Browse your snapshot history
3. Click any snapshot to view details
4. Use "Snap Back" to restore

[Open SnapBack View](command:workbench.view.extension.snapback)

media.markdown:
## Viewing Your Snapshots

### SnapBack Sidebar
Click the SnapBack icon (🧢) in the Activity Bar to see:
- All your snapshots in chronological order
- File-specific snapshot history
- Protected files list

### Timeline View
VS Code's built-in Timeline view shows:
- File history at a glance
- Quick restore options
- Diff preview

**Keyboard Shortcut:** `Ctrl+Alt+Z` (or `Cmd+Alt+Z`) to Snap Back

steps.title: Bonus: Team Protection Policies
steps.description:
Share protection policies with your team using `.snapbackrc` configuration:

1. Create `.snapbackrc` in your repo root
2. Add patterns with protection levels
3. Commit to Git

**Everyone on your team gets the same protection automatically.**

media.markdown:
## Team Configuration Example

Create `.snapbackrc` in your project root:

``````

**Benefits:**
- Consistent protection across the team
- New team members get protection automatically
- Reduce accidental breaking changes
